stimulus: All employees who complete the certification program receive a pay raise. Every member of the marketing department has completed the certification program. Janet is a member of the marketing department.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Janet has received a pay raise.
choice_b: Only marketing department employees complete the certification program.
choice_c: Janet is the most qualified member of the marketing department.
choice_d: The certification program is required for all employees.
choice_e: Janet will be promoted after receiving her pay raise.
explanation: Janet is in the marketing department, all marketing members completed the certification, and all who complete the certification get a raise. Therefore Janet must have received a pay raise.
explanation_a: Correct. Janet is in marketing, so she completed the certification, so she received a pay raise. This follows by two applications of the conditional chain.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says all marketing members completed it, but does not say only marketing members did. Others outside marketing may have completed it too.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Nothing in the stimulus addresses qualifications or relative competence among department members.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says all marketing members completed it, not that it is required for all employees company-wide.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus guarantees a pay raise, not a promotion. This goes beyond what the premises support.

stimulus: Every bird in the sanctuary is tagged with a GPS-enabled band for tracking migration patterns and territorial behavior. All tagged animals in the sanctuary are monitored daily by a dedicated team of wildlife researchers who record feeding and nesting data. The peregrine falcon is a bird that has resided in the sanctuary since last spring.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: The peregrine falcon is the only bird monitored daily.
choice_b: All animals in the sanctuary are birds.
choice_c: Researchers monitor only birds in the sanctuary.
choice_d: The peregrine falcon is monitored daily by researchers.
choice_e: Tagging is the most effective method for tracking birds.
explanation: The falcon is a bird in the sanctuary, so it is tagged. All tagged animals are monitored daily. Therefore the falcon is monitored daily.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Other birds and tagged animals may also be monitored daily. The stimulus does not single out the falcon.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions tagged animals generally, implying there could be non-bird animals in the sanctuary.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Other tagged animals that are not birds would also be monitored daily according to the stimulus.
explanation_d: Correct. The falcon is a bird in the sanctuary, so it is tagged, and all tagged animals are monitored daily. The chain guarantees daily monitoring.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus describes the sanctuary's practice but makes no comparative claim about the effectiveness of tagging.

stimulus: Any student who earns an A in the seminar is invited to present at the conference. No student who misses more than two classes earns an A in the seminar. David missed four classes this semester.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: David will not be invited to present at the conference.
choice_b: David is not a good student.
choice_c: Students who attend every class earn an A.
choice_d: David missed more classes than any other student.
choice_e: The conference only features students who earned an A.
explanation: David missed four classes, which is more than two. Therefore he did not earn an A. Only those who earn an A are invited to present. So David is not invited.
explanation_a: Correct. David missed more than two classes, so he did not earn an A. Since only A-earners are invited to present, David will not be invited.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Missing classes and not earning an A in one seminar does not establish that David is not a good student overall.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says missing more than two classes prevents an A, but perfect attendance does not guarantee an A.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says nothing about how many classes other students missed.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says A-earners are invited, but does not say the conference excludes all others or features only students.

stimulus: All paintings displayed in the west wing of the museum were created before 1900. The portrait of Countess Lieven is displayed in the west wing. Any painting created before 1900 is classified as a historical work by the museum's cataloging system.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: The portrait of Countess Lieven is the oldest painting in the west wing.
choice_b: The museum's cataloging system classifies all portraits as historical works.
choice_c: Only historical works are displayed in the west wing.
choice_d: The portrait of Countess Lieven is classified as a historical work.
choice_e: Paintings created after 1900 are not classified as historical works.
explanation: The portrait is in the west wing, so it was created before 1900. Paintings created before 1900 are classified as historical works. Therefore the portrait is classified as a historical work.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus does not provide information about the relative age of paintings in the west wing.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The classification depends on the date of creation, not on whether the painting is a portrait.
explanation_c: Incorrect. While west wing paintings are pre-1900 and thus historical works, there could be non-historical works displayed there for other reasons not addressed by the stimulus.
explanation_d: Correct. The portrait is in the west wing, so it predates 1900, and all pre-1900 paintings are classified as historical works.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says pre-1900 paintings are historical works but does not say post-1900 paintings cannot also be classified that way.

stimulus: If a contract is not signed by both parties, it is not legally binding. The lease agreement between Torres and Blakely was not signed by Blakely. A contract that is not legally binding cannot be enforced in court.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Blakely intended to breach the lease agreement.
choice_b: Torres should not have entered into the agreement.
choice_c: The lease agreement between Torres and Blakely cannot be enforced in court.
choice_d: No lease agreement is legally binding unless both parties sign it.
choice_e: Blakely is not obligated to pay rent under any circumstances.
explanation: The agreement was not signed by both parties (Blakely did not sign). So it is not legally binding. Contracts that are not legally binding cannot be enforced in court. Therefore this lease cannot be enforced in court.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about Blakely's intentions or motivations.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses legal enforceability, not whether Torres should have entered the agreement.
explanation_c: Correct. Blakely did not sign, so the contract was not signed by both parties. Therefore it is not legally binding, and therefore it cannot be enforced in court.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus states this rule for contracts generally, not exclusively for lease agreements. Moreover, it already follows from the premises as stated.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses court enforcement of this specific contract, not Blakely's obligations under all circumstances.

stimulus: Every planet in the Kepler-442 system that has a measurable atmospheric envelope also has confirmed evidence of surface water based on spectroscopic analysis. Kepler-442b has a thick nitrogen-rich atmosphere that was detected by orbital telescope surveys. No planet in that system without surface water can sustain microbial life according to current astrobiological models.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Kepler-442b supports microbial life.
choice_b: All planets with surface water support microbial life.
choice_c: Kepler-442b is the only planet in the system with an atmosphere.
choice_d: Surface water is sufficient for supporting microbial life.
choice_e: Kepler-442b has surface water.
explanation: Kepler-442b has an atmosphere and is in the Kepler-442 system. Every planet in that system with an atmosphere has surface water. Therefore Kepler-442b has surface water.
explanation_a: Incorrect. We know it has surface water, but having surface water is necessary for microbial life, not sufficient. We cannot conclude it supports life.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says surface water is necessary for microbial life, not that it is sufficient.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about whether other planets in the system have atmospheres.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus states surface water is necessary for microbial life, not that it guarantees microbial life.
explanation_e: Correct. Kepler-442b has an atmosphere and is in the system, so by the first premise, it must have surface water.

stimulus: Any book that wins the Whitmore Prize is added to the university's permanent collection. The novel Tidewater won the Whitmore Prize last year. Every book in the university's permanent collection is available for interlibrary loan.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: Tidewater is the best novel in the permanent collection.
choice_b: Only Whitmore Prize winners are in the permanent collection.
choice_c: Tidewater is available for interlibrary loan.
choice_d: Every novel available for interlibrary loan has won the Whitmore Prize.
choice_e: The Whitmore Prize is awarded only to novels.
explanation: Tidewater won the Whitmore Prize, so it is in the permanent collection. Everything in the permanent collection is available for interlibrary loan. Therefore Tidewater is available for interlibrary loan.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus makes no qualitative comparisons among books in the collection.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says prize winners are added, but other books could be in the collection for different reasons.
explanation_c: Correct. Winning the prize puts Tidewater in the permanent collection, and all permanent collection books are available for interlibrary loan.
explanation_d: Incorrect. This reverses the conditional. Books can be available for loan without having won the prize.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions that Tidewater is a novel, but does not restrict the prize to novels.

stimulus: No animal classified as endangered by the wildlife commission may be commercially hunted. The eastern gray wolf has been classified as endangered by the wildlife commission. Any species that cannot be commercially hunted receives automatic habitat protections.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: The eastern gray wolf receives automatic habitat protections.
choice_b: All species with habitat protections are endangered.
choice_c: The eastern gray wolf is the most threatened species in its region.
choice_d: Commercial hunting is the primary threat to the eastern gray wolf.
choice_e: Habitat protections are sufficient to save the eastern gray wolf.
explanation: The wolf is endangered, so it cannot be commercially hunted. Species that cannot be commercially hunted receive habitat protections. Therefore the wolf receives habitat protections.
explanation_a: Correct. Endangered status prohibits commercial hunting, and prohibition of commercial hunting triggers automatic habitat protections.
explanation_b: Incorrect. This reverses the reasoning. Non-endangered species might also be unable to be commercially hunted for other reasons and thus receive protections.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no comparative information about threat levels among species.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus does not identify what threatens the wolf, only that its endangered status prohibits hunting.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says the wolf receives protections but makes no claim about whether those protections are sufficient.

stimulus: All members of the city council must disclose their financial interests before voting on zoning matters. Councilmember Reeves has not disclosed her financial interests. A vote on the new commercial zoning ordinance is scheduled for Tuesday.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Councilmember Reeves opposes the new commercial zoning ordinance.
choice_b: The vote on the zoning ordinance will be delayed.
choice_c: Councilmember Reeves has a conflict of interest regarding the ordinance.
choice_d: Councilmember Reeves may not vote on the zoning ordinance unless she discloses her financial interests.
choice_e: Other council members have already disclosed their financial interests.
explanation: Council members must disclose financial interests before voting on zoning matters. Since Reeves has not disclosed, she cannot vote on the zoning ordinance until she does.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about Reeves's position on the ordinance.
explanation_b: Incorrect. One member's failure to disclose does not necessarily delay the entire vote.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The disclosure requirement applies to all members regardless of whether they have conflicts of interest.
explanation_d: Correct. Disclosure is required before voting on zoning matters. Since Reeves has not disclosed, she may not vote unless she first discloses.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says nothing about whether other members have completed their disclosures.

stimulus: Researchers found that every participant who slept fewer than six hours performed poorly on the memory test. Carlos slept only four hours the night before the test. No participant who performed poorly on the memory test was selected for the advanced study group.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Carlos was not selected for the advanced study group.
choice_b: Sleeping more than six hours guarantees good performance on the memory test.
choice_c: Carlos performed the worst of all participants.
choice_d: The advanced study group includes only participants who slept more than six hours.
choice_e: Carlos could improve his performance by sleeping more.
explanation: Carlos slept four hours, which is fewer than six. So he performed poorly. No one who performed poorly was selected for the advanced group. Therefore Carlos was not selected.
explanation_a: Correct. Carlos slept fewer than six hours, so he performed poorly, and poor performers were not selected for the advanced study group.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says fewer than six hours leads to poor performance, but does not say more than six hours guarantees good performance.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says Carlos performed poorly, not that he performed the worst among all participants.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Some participants might have slept six or more hours but still performed poorly for other reasons and been excluded.
explanation_e: Incorrect. While plausible, this is a causal prediction not guaranteed by the stimulus, which reports correlation in this study.

stimulus: If a manuscript is accepted by the editorial board, it is published in the quarterly journal. The editorial board did not accept Dr. Farrow's manuscript. Only published works are eligible for the Bancroft Award.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Dr. Farrow's manuscript is not eligible for the Bancroft Award.
choice_b: Dr. Farrow's manuscript will never be published.
choice_c: The editorial board's standards are too high.
choice_d: Dr. Farrow's manuscript was of poor quality.
choice_e: All manuscripts published in the journal are eligible for the Bancroft Award.
explanation: Accepted manuscripts are published in the journal. Published works are eligible for the Bancroft Award. So any manuscript published in the journal is eligible for the award.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Although the editorial board did not accept it, the manuscript could be published elsewhere and thus still be eligible for the Bancroft Award.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The manuscript was not accepted by this board, but it could be published through other means.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no basis for evaluating the appropriateness of the board's standards.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus gives no reason for the rejection. Many factors besides quality could be involved.
explanation_e: Correct. Accepted manuscripts are published in the journal, and published works are eligible for the Bancroft Award. So all journal-published manuscripts are eligible.

stimulus: Every applicant who passes the bar exam in State X is eligible for licensure. No one is eligible for licensure in State X without completing a background check. Thompson passed the bar exam in State X last month.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: Thompson has already completed a background check.
choice_b: Thompson is eligible for licensure in State X.
choice_c: Thompson must complete a background check to be eligible for licensure.
choice_d: Everyone who completes a background check is eligible for licensure.
choice_e: Thompson will practice law in State X.
explanation: Thompson passed the bar, so he is eligible for licensure. But eligibility requires completing a background check. Therefore Thompson must complete one to be eligible.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says Thompson passed the bar and is thus eligible, but does not confirm whether the background check has been completed yet.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Passing the bar makes Thompson eligible, but eligibility also requires a background check, which may not be completed yet.
explanation_c: Correct. Since licensure eligibility requires a background check, and Thompson has passed the bar making him eligible contingent on that requirement, he must complete it.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Completing a background check is necessary for eligibility, not sufficient. The stimulus does not say it alone guarantees eligibility.
explanation_e: Incorrect. Eligibility for licensure does not guarantee that Thompson will actually practice law.

stimulus: All flowering plants in the alpine meadow require pollination by insects. No insect pollinators are active when temperatures drop below freezing. Temperatures in the alpine meadow dropped below freezing last week and have not risen since.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: All flowering plants in the alpine meadow have died.
choice_b: No flowering plants in the alpine meadow are currently being pollinated by insects.
choice_c: Insect pollinators have left the alpine meadow permanently.
choice_d: Flowering plants cannot survive below-freezing temperatures.
choice_e: The alpine meadow will have fewer flowers next season.
explanation: Temperatures have been below freezing since last week. No insect pollinators are active below freezing. So currently no insects are pollinating, meaning no flowering plants there are being pollinated by insects.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says they require insect pollination but does not say that a period without pollination kills the plants.
explanation_b: Correct. Since temperatures are below freezing and insect pollinators are inactive below freezing, no insect pollination is currently occurring in the meadow.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus says pollinators are inactive below freezing, not that they leave permanently. They may return when it warms.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses pollination requirements, not the plants' ability to survive cold temperatures.
explanation_e: Incorrect. This is a prediction that goes beyond what the stimulus supports. The plants may recover when temperatures rise.

stimulus: In Greendale, every household that subscribes to the local newspaper also receives the weekly advertising circular. The Petersons subscribe to the local newspaper. Any household that receives the weekly advertising circular is eligible for the merchant discount program.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Most households in Greendale subscribe to the local newspaper.
choice_b: Only newspaper subscribers are eligible for the merchant discount program.
choice_c: The Petersons subscribe to the newspaper primarily for the advertising circular.
choice_d: The Petersons are eligible for the merchant discount program.
choice_e: The merchant discount program is available only through the advertising circular.
explanation: The Petersons subscribe to the newspaper, so they receive the circular. Households receiving the circular are eligible for the discount program. Therefore the Petersons are eligible.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions only the Petersons' subscription and provides no data about subscription rates generally.
explanation_b: Incorrect. Other households might receive the circular through means other than a newspaper subscription.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about the Petersons' motivations for subscribing.
explanation_d: Correct. Newspaper subscription leads to the circular, and the circular leads to discount program eligibility. The Petersons subscribe, so they are eligible.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says circular recipients are eligible, but does not say the circular is the only path to eligibility.

stimulus: All symphonies performed at the Ravenswood Festival are rehearsed for at least three weeks. Any piece rehearsed for at least three weeks is performed from memory by the orchestra. The festival will perform Dvořák's New World Symphony this season.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Dvořák's New World Symphony is the most rehearsed piece at the festival.
choice_b: The orchestra will perform Dvořák's New World Symphony from memory.
choice_c: Three weeks of rehearsal is the minimum for any orchestral performance.
choice_d: Dvořák's New World Symphony has been performed at the festival before.
choice_e: Only symphonies are performed at the Ravenswood Festival.
explanation: The symphony will be performed at the festival, so it is rehearsed for at least three weeks. Pieces rehearsed for at least three weeks are performed from memory. Therefore it will be performed from memory.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus says it will be rehearsed for at least three weeks but provides no comparison with other pieces.
explanation_b: Correct. Festival symphonies get three-plus weeks of rehearsal, and three-plus weeks means performance from memory. So the New World Symphony will be performed from memory.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The three-week minimum applies to pieces at the Ravenswood Festival, not to orchestral performances generally.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says it will be performed this season but provides no historical information about past performances.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus mentions symphonies performed at the festival but does not exclude other types of pieces.

stimulus: If a defendant is found guilty of a felony, the judge must impose a sentence of at least one year. Robinson was found guilty of a felony. Any sentence of at least one year requires a mandatory review by the appellate division.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Robinson will appeal the conviction.
choice_b: Robinson's sentence will be reviewed by the appellate division.
choice_c: The judge will impose the maximum sentence on Robinson.
choice_d: Robinson committed a violent crime.
choice_e: The appellate division will reduce Robinson's sentence.
explanation: Robinson was found guilty of a felony, so the judge must impose at least one year. A sentence of at least one year requires mandatory appellate review. So Robinson's sentence will be reviewed.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The review is mandatory and automatic; it does not depend on Robinson choosing to appeal.
explanation_b: Correct. Felony conviction requires at least one year, and at least one year triggers mandatory appellate review. So Robinson's sentence must be reviewed.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus requires a minimum of one year but says nothing about whether the judge will impose the maximum.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says Robinson committed a felony but provides no information about whether it was violent.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus requires a review, but does not specify what the outcome of the review will be.

stimulus: All deep-sea fish in the Mariana Trench have bioluminescent organs. Any organism with bioluminescent organs produces its own light in the absence of sunlight. The anglerfish is a deep-sea fish found in the Mariana Trench.
stem: Which one of the following can be properly inferred from the information above?
choice_a: The anglerfish is the only bioluminescent fish in the Mariana Trench.
choice_b: Bioluminescence is found only in deep-sea environments.
choice_c: The anglerfish produces its own light in the absence of sunlight.
choice_d: All organisms that produce light are found in the Mariana Trench.
choice_e: The anglerfish uses bioluminescence to attract prey.
explanation: The anglerfish is a deep-sea fish in the Mariana Trench, so it has bioluminescent organs. Organisms with such organs produce their own light without sunlight. So the anglerfish produces its own light.
explanation_a: Incorrect. All deep-sea fish there have bioluminescent organs, so there could be many such fish, not just the anglerfish.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus describes the Mariana Trench but makes no claim about where else bioluminescence occurs.
explanation_c: Correct. The anglerfish is in the Trench, so it has bioluminescent organs, so it produces its own light in the absence of sunlight.
explanation_d: Incorrect. This reverses and overgeneralizes the conditional. Light-producing organisms may exist elsewhere.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says the anglerfish produces light but provides no information about the purpose of its bioluminescence.

stimulus: Every voter in the precinct who registered before the deadline received a mail-in ballot. Garcia registered before the deadline. No one who did not receive a mail-in ballot can vote by mail in the upcoming election.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Garcia will vote by mail in the upcoming election.
choice_b: Only voters who registered before the deadline can vote in the election.
choice_c: Garcia lives in the precinct.
choice_d: Everyone who received a mail-in ballot will use it.
choice_e: Garcia received a mail-in ballot.
explanation: Garcia registered before the deadline. Every voter in the precinct who registered before the deadline received a mail-in ballot. Assuming Garcia is a voter in the precinct, Garcia received a mail-in ballot.
explanation_a: Incorrect. Garcia received a ballot, but the stimulus does not say Garcia will actually use it to vote by mail.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus addresses mail-in ballots for those who registered early but does not restrict all voting to those who registered before the deadline.
explanation_c: Incorrect. This is presupposed by the context but not explicitly stated as a conclusion to be drawn.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Receiving a ballot does not guarantee that the recipient will use it.
explanation_e: Correct. Garcia registered before the deadline and is a voter in the precinct, so Garcia received a mail-in ballot.

stimulus: All plays written by Marlowe are included in the Elizabethan canon. Any work in the Elizabethan canon is studied in the department's survey course. Doctor Faustus is a play written by Marlowe.
stem: Which one of the following conclusions is best supported by the statements above?
choice_a: Doctor Faustus is the most important work in the Elizabethan canon.
choice_b: Only plays by Marlowe are in the Elizabethan canon.
choice_c: Marlowe is the most studied Elizabethan playwright.
choice_d: The survey course covers only Elizabethan works.
choice_e: Doctor Faustus is studied in the department's survey course.
explanation: Doctor Faustus was written by Marlowe, so it is in the Elizabethan canon. Works in the canon are studied in the survey course. Therefore Doctor Faustus is studied in the survey course.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus includes Doctor Faustus in the canon but makes no claim about its relative importance.
explanation_b: Incorrect. The stimulus says Marlowe's plays are in the canon, but other works could be in it as well.
explanation_c: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no comparative information about how much various playwrights are studied.
explanation_d: Incorrect. The stimulus says canon works are studied in the course, but the course could include non-Elizabethan works too.
explanation_e: Correct. Marlowe wrote it, so it is in the canon, and canon works are studied in the survey course.

stimulus: Any employee classified as a remote worker must submit weekly activity reports. Jensen is classified as a remote worker. No employee who fails to submit weekly activity reports is eligible for the quarterly performance bonus.
stem: If the statements above are true, which one of the following must also be true?
choice_a: Jensen is the only remote worker at the company.
choice_b: Jensen must submit weekly activity reports to remain eligible for the quarterly performance bonus.
choice_c: All employees must submit weekly activity reports.
choice_d: Remote workers are never eligible for the quarterly performance bonus.
choice_e: Jensen has already submitted weekly activity reports.
explanation: Jensen is a remote worker, so she must submit weekly activity reports. Employees who fail to submit these reports are ineligible for the bonus. Therefore Jensen must submit them to remain eligible.
explanation_a: Incorrect. The stimulus provides no information about how many remote workers exist at the company.
explanation_b: Correct. Jensen must submit reports because she is a remote worker, and failing to submit makes one ineligible for the bonus.
explanation_c: Incorrect. Only remote workers are required to submit weekly reports; other employees may not have this requirement.
explanation_d: Incorrect. Remote workers who submit their reports would remain eligible for the bonus.
explanation_e: Incorrect. The stimulus says Jensen must submit reports but does not confirm whether she has already done so.
